name: Hot Semi-Arid (Steppe)
description: Hot dry summers, mild winters, low annual rainfall (200–500mm). Clay or sandy soils.
mainChallenge: Extreme summer heat and drought. Overwatering in clay soil kills roots.

name: Hot Desert
description: Extreme heat, very low rainfall (<250mm), high evaporation. Sandy or rocky soils.
mainChallenge: Almost zero rainfall. Trees must be heavily irrigated for years before self-sufficient.

name: Tropical Savanna
description: Hot year-round with a distinct wet and dry season. Rich laterite or clay soils.
mainChallenge: Intense dry season — young trees need irrigation until established.
bestWindow: Start of wet season (varies by hemisphere)

name: Mediterranean (Hot Summer)
description: Hot dry summers, mild wet winters. Thin rocky or limestone soils.
mainChallenge: Summer drought — trees must survive 3–4 dry months. Avoid wet clay planting.

name: Mediterranean (Warm Summer)
description: Warm dry summers, cool wet winters. Milder than Csa. Good for a wide range of trees.
mainChallenge: Summer dry period, though less severe than hot Mediterranean.

name: Temperate Oceanic
description: Mild summers, cool winters, rain distributed year-round. Rich loamy soils.
mainChallenge: Waterlogged soils in winter. Choose well-drained spots for deep-rooted species.

name: Humid Subtropical
description: Hot humid summers, mild winters, rain year-round. Fertile alluvial or clay soils.
mainChallenge: Humidity brings fungal disease. Ensure good air circulation and drainage.

name: Humid Continental (Warm Summer)
description: Warm summers, cold winters with significant snowfall. Deep fertile soils.
mainChallenge: Hard frost. Use frost-hardy species and avoid planting too late in autumn.
bestWindow: Apr–May (after last frost) or Sep (before first frost)

name: Subarctic / Boreal
description: Short cool summers, very long cold winters. Acidic thin soils over permafrost.
mainChallenge: Permafrost limits root depth. Short growing season. Only the hardiest species survive.
